A 5th‑level slot is two levels above the spell’s base level (3rd).  
Fireball normally deals **8d6** fire damage, and it increases by **1d6** for each slot level above 3rd.  

So at 5th level: **8d6 + 2d6 = 10d6** fire damage (in a 20‑foot radius).

A Fireball cast at a 5th‑level spell slot deals **10d6** fire damage (8d6 base + 2d6 for the two levels above 3rd).  
So the damage ranges from **10** to **60** points, in a 20‑foot radius sphere.
A Fireball spell cast with a 5th-level spell slot does **9d6** fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
A 5th-level Fireball deals 9d6 fire damage. (8d6 base + 1d6 for the 4th level, and another +1d6 for the 5th level).
At 3rd level, **Magic Missile creates 5 darts** (3 base darts + 1 extra dart for each level above 1st).

At 3rd level you get **five** darts.  
Magic Missile creates 3 darts at 1st level, and you add one extra dart for each slot level above 1st—so 3 + (3 – 1) = 5.
When cast at 3rd level, Magic Missile creates **4 darts**.

`Magic Missile` creates **five darts** when cast at 3rd level.

Each dart deals 1d4 + 1 force damage to its target. The darts all strike simultaneously, and you can direct them to hit one creature or several.

Aragorn isn’t an official 5e character, so the rules don’t give him a set level.  
If you want him to be a playable or NPC hero in your game, treat him as a **high‑level human fighter/ranger**—usually **level 18–20**.  Give him the same ability score spread and feats that reflect his skills (e.g., *Dual Wielder*, *Great Weapon Master*, *Sentinel*, etc.) and a weapon‑proficiency set that matches his swords and bows.  That will approximate the “Lord of the Rings” Aragorn in a 5e setting.

Aragorn isn’t a built‑in D&D 5e character, so he has no official “level” in the rules.  
If you want to run him in a game, you’ll assign a level that matches his lore‑based power.

Cure Wounds heals **1d8 + your spellcasting modifier**.  
With a +3 modifier that’s **1d8 + 3**, so Aragorn recovers **4 to 11 hit points** (unless his hit‑point maximum caps it). If you upcast it, add another 1d8 for each slot above 1st level.
